articles.title: RUMOR: SMALLER DYNAMIC ISLAND BUT NO UNDER-SCREEN FACE ID FOR IPHONE 18
articles.summary: The iPhone 18 is rumored to debut a slimmer, narrower Dynamic Island, marking its first design change since the iPhone 14 Pro. While it won't yet adopt under-screen Face ID or a hidden front camera, the refined shape would make the cutout less visually dominant. This adjustment pushes Apple closer to its all-screen design goal and offers a cleaner look that frees up more space for both the display and status bar.

articles.title: HIGGSFIELD: $50 MILLION SERIES A RAISED TO TRANSFORM AI VIDEO CREATION
articles.summary: Higgsfield.ai raised $50 million in Series A funding led by GFT Ventures to advance its AI video creation platform. The company has gained 11 million users and 1.2 billion social media impressions in five months since launch, outpacing competitors with its Click-to-Video technology that creates cinematic clips with a single click. The funding will support enterprise platform expansion, team growth, and accelerated market strategy in the $600 billion short-form video market.

articles.title: MIDJOURNEY'S STYLE EXPLORER V1
articles.summary: Midjourney launched an early version of its style explorer that features thumbnails that show prompts rendered with different SREF codes sampling from the continuous latent space of styles. Users can save their preferred styles, try out styles with custom prompts by hovering, and use fuzzy search to find styles matching descriptors like "photographic" or "anime." The tool allows rapid style experimentation by applying different visual aesthetics to the same prompt across multiple variations.

articles.title: EVERYONE WANTS A FASTER HORSE. WE AIM TO PLEASE
articles.summary: Perception often shapes value and experience more strongly than objective improvements. Since user-reported data is biased and incomplete, blending attitudes, behaviors, and latent knowledge offers deeper insights. Examples like Starbucks' handcrafted image, eco-friendly dish soap, and GE's playful MRI rooms show how subtle, perception-driven design can create more meaningful and empathetic outcomes.

articles.title: WHY JAGUAR'S RIGHT TO STICK TO ITS CONTROVERSIAL REBRAND
articles.summary: Jaguar's controversial rebrand, launched with bold concept cars in striking colors, deliberately distanced the brand from traditional buyers to target a younger, wealthier, design-focused audience. While sales have dipped, Jaguar is embracing exclusivity with cars priced over $130,000. It claims that rising interest among high-income and younger consumers is proof that the polarizing strategy is working.

articles.title: LESSONS LEARNED IN AI AND DESIGN WORKFLOWS
articles.summary: A Dropbox product designer shares insights from experimenting with AI tools like Figma Make and Cursor. They found that while these tools show promise, the gap between design and engineering remains significant. AI tools actually expose design system weaknesses rather than hiding them, making strong foundational systems more critical for successful implementation. The key to meaningful AI adoption lies in having internal champions who provide resources and support for experimentation. Critical thinking remains essential, as AI outputs still lack crucial context about product strategy and user insights.

articles.title: ALL-IN-ONE AI GENERATOR
articles.summary: PXZ AI is your all-in-one free AI creative platform, excelling in video generation and graphic design.

articles.title: CURATED SET OF FUTURISTIC UI COMPONENTS
articles.summary: Cosmic UI is a set of beautifully-designed sci-fi components with customizable SVG shapes. Built for modern frameworks with accessible interactive logic.

articles.title: TRANSFORM YOUR PICTURE TO DRAWING IN SECONDS
articles.summary: Use AI to transform any picture into pencil sketches, watercolor paintings, and artistic drawings.

articles.title: SEVEN IPHONE AIR DETAILS YOU MIGHT'VE MISSED
articles.summary: Apple's new iPhone Air pushes design into ultra-thin territory but comes with notable trade-offs. Its Dynamic Island sits deeper into the display to accommodate packed components, the USB-C port is off-center, and the phone uses a single rear camera and mono speaker, giving it a more minimal—but compromised—look. These changes create a sleeker silhouette overall, but at the cost of symmetry, audio fullness, and advanced camera design.

articles.title: SMASH TAKES DOWN WETRANSFER IN THIS CHEEKY BUT CLEVER CAMPAIGN
articles.summary: Smash capitalized on WeTransfer's AI-related backlash with a cheeky, humorous campaign positioning itself as the ethical alternative. By emphasizing user privacy, intellectual property protection, and transparency—including showing the environmental cost of transfers—Smash attracted a surge of new users globally. The campaign proves that well-timed humor combined with genuine values can turn a competitor's misstep into a major growth opportunity.

articles.title: HOW LEADING AGENCIES ARE NAVIGATING THE NEW ERA OF BRAND STORYTELLING
articles.summary: Three leading agency executives discussed how modern brand storytelling has evolved beyond traditional content creation to become an integral part of brand building across multiple channels and formats. Agencies are evolving from simple content creators to strategic partners, collaborating closely with increasingly capable in-house teams. They focus on modular content systems that serve business objectives rather than just creative expression. AI should be embraced as a creative enhancement tool that accelerates ideation and removes barriers, while agencies must also adapt their content strategies for AI-powered search engines that prioritize clear, authoritative answers to audience questions.

articles.title: EXPERT TIPS ON GETTING INTO CREATIVE FLOW (AND STAYING THERE)
articles.summary: It is important to work with natural energy rhythms and create dedicated time slots for deep work.

articles.title: THE BIGGEST REDESIGN FLOPS OF ALL TIME
articles.summary: Cracker Barrel's recent logo redesign sparked such fierce consumer backlash that the company immediately reverted to its original design and suspended a $700 million rebrand plan.
